title: Add PWA support and fix live step counter in Chrome

requirements.summary: Add full PWA support with manifest, service worker, and home screen installation capability
acceptanceCriteria: A valid manifest.json is served and linked in index.html with name, icons, start_url, display='standalone', theme_color, and background_color fields
A service worker is registered that caches the app shell (HTML, JS, CSS) so the app loads when launched from the home screen
Chrome's 'Add to Home Screen' prompt or menu option successfully adds the app icon to the home screen
Tapping the home screen icon opens the app in standalone mode (no browser chrome/address bar) without a blank screen or error

file_operations.description: Create a web app manifest with name 'FinFit Tracker', short_name 'FinFit', start_url '/', display 'standalone', theme_color matching the primary brand color, background_color matching the app background, and icons array including the generated PWA icon at frontend/public/assets/generated/pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png in multiple sizes (192x192, 512x512). Set appropriate purpose flags for icons.

Create a service worker that implements install, activate, and fetch event handlers. Cache the app shell (index.html, main bundle JS/CSS, and critical assets) during the install event. Use a cache-first strategy for static assets and network-first for API calls to the ICP backend. Ensure Internet Identity authentication redirects and canister calls are not intercepted or cached. Include cache versioning and cleanup of old caches during activation.

Add a <link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json'> tag in the <head> section to link the web app manifest. Add theme-color meta tag matching the manifest theme_color. Ensure the file references the generated PWA icon at frontend/public/assets/generated/pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png as an apple-touch-icon for iOS compatibility (even though iOS support is out of scope, this improves cross-platform appearance).

Register the service worker at the top level after the DOM is loaded. Use navigator.serviceWorker.register('/service-worker.js') with proper error handling. Log registration success/failure to the console. Ensure registration does not block app startup or interfere with Internet Identity flows.

requirements.summary: Fix live step counter to detect and count steps correctly in Chrome on Android with proper DeviceMotion permission handling
acceptanceCriteria: The step counter requests DeviceMotion permission in Chrome when the user starts tracking, displaying a permission prompt if required
Steps are counted and the count increments in real time on Chrome for Android when the device is moved/walked with
The StepTrackerWidget shows the live step count updating as steps are taken
If permission is denied, a clear message is shown to the user explaining that motion permission is needed for step tracking
The step count persists to localStorage and auto-saves as before

file_operations.description: Update the useStepTracker hook to request DeviceMotion permission in Chrome before registering the devicemotion event listener. Check if DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission exists (iOS/some Android browsers) and call it when starting tracking. Handle permission grant/denial states. If permission is denied, set an error state that the UI can display. Ensure the step detection algorithm processes Chrome's DeviceMotion event data correctly (event.accelerationIncludingGravity or event.acceleration). Refine the threshold logic if needed to detect steps reliably on Chrome for Android. Preserve existing localStorage persistence and auto-save behavior.

Update the StepTrackerWidget component to display permission status and error messages from the useStepTracker hook. Show a clear user-friendly message if DeviceMotion permission is denied (e.g., 'Motion permission is required for step tracking. Please enable it in your browser settings.'). Ensure the start tracking button triggers the permission request flow. Display the live step count prominently and ensure the progress bar updates in real time as steps are detected. Maintain the existing 10,000-step goal visualization.
